text: The cartographer arrived in the village the same week the river changed its mind. This was how the elders described it, that the water had simply reconsidered, had looked at the limestone gorge it had carved across seventeen thousand years and found the whole project somehow insufficient, and so had begun, almost imperceptibly at first, to lean elsewhere. She came with her instruments of brass and leather, her habit of measuring everything twice and trusting neither measurement, and she set up her drafting table in the widow Maret's spare room where the wallpaper peeled in long questioning curls, as if the house itself had been posing some unanswerable inquiry for decades and was only now showing the strain of it. Outside her window, the river moved with the slow conviction of something that had already made its decision and was merely enacting the consequences, indifferent to the maps she would make of where it used to be.
